
# PCA Diagnostics {#sec-pca-diagnostics}

The following table summarises the variance explained by PC1 in each PCA run:

```{r}
#| label: setup-pca-diagnostics
#| include: false
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-pca-summary
#| tbl-cap: "PCA diagnostics — variance explained by PC1 (indicative values; run 05_IMPTcalculator.R for up-to-date results)"

# This table would be populated from the PCA variance report CSV once available
# Placeholder structure:
pca_summary <- data.frame(
    Dimension_Mode = c(
        "Accessibility (global)", "Mobility (global)", "Safety (global)",
        "Accessibility – Car", "Accessibility – PT", "Accessibility – Bike", "Accessibility – Walk",
        "Mobility – Car", "Mobility – PT", "Mobility – Bike", "Mobility – Walk",
        "Safety – Car", "Safety – Bike", "Safety – Walk"
    ),
    PC1_Variance = c(
        "[run script]", "[run script]", "[run script]",
        "[run script]", "[run script]", "[run script]", "[run script]",
        "[run script]", "[run script]", "[run script]", "[run script]",
        "[run script]", "[run script]", "[run script]"
    ),
    Top_Variable = rep("[run script]", 14)
)

kable(pca_summary,
    col.names = c("Dimension / Mode", "PC1 Variance (%)", "Top contributing variable"),
    booktabs = TRUE
) |>
    kable_styling(font_size = 10, full_width = FALSE)
```

::: {.callout-tip}
To populate this table automatically, source `05_IMPTcalculator.R` and read `PCA_Variance_Table.csv` and `PCA_Variance_and_Correlations.csv` from the results folder.
:::
